strategy_criteria_summary: USA optionable stocks over $20 with market cap above $2B, 3M average volume above 1M, beta above 1, price above the 50/200-day SMAs, within 20% of the 52-week high, and RSI 40-70. This keeps the structure-driven pool liquid and tradeable without over-filtering it to zero.
scoring_reasons: trend alignment was mixed rather than decisive|relative strength was clearly supportive|market/sector environment was clearly supportive|best contract was long_call 2026-05-15 67.5|News flow was mixed rather than one-way.|Spread is wider than ideal but still usable.
decision_reasoning: IONQ has the cleanest directional setup in the bundle: massive relative strength vs SPY (+72%) and sector (+59%), strong 20d/50d momentum, and a confirmed earnings catalyst on 2026-05-07. However, implied volatility at ~153% makes the long call extremely vulnerable to post-earnings IV crush, and the stock is extended after an 80% run, raising the odds of a 'beat-and-fade'. CSCO's chosen strike is far OTM with a weak catalyst profile, CRWV's only listed strike ($200 vs $131 spot) is a lottery ticket, and SWKS appears to have already reported with soft Q3 guidance. IONQ is the best single setup but the premium/IV risk keeps it below the recommend threshold — monitor for an IV pullback or dip toward the 51 strike before entering.
